clusters.title: Watch: Philippine senator chased by police after arrest warrant issued
clusters.neutral_headline: Lockdown in Philippine Senate after gunshots fired
bullet_summary: Reported by NBC World, BBC World, France24 and others

items.title: Watch: Philippine senator chased by police after arrest warrant issued
items.standfirst: Surveillance footage captured the moment law enforcement agents chased Philippine Senator Ronald dela Rosa inside the Philippine Senate building. The Senator has an outstanding arrest warrant for crimes against humanity for overseeing former President Duterte's war on drugs.
items.content: Surveillance footage captured the moment law enforcement agents chased Philippine Senator Ronald dela Rosa inside the Philippine Senate building. The Senator has an outstanding arrest warrant for crimes against humanity for overseeing former President Duterte's war on drugs.

items.title: Lockdown in Philippine Senate after gunshots fired
items.standfirst: Police commandos entered the building where a senator wanted by the International Criminal Court had sought refuge.
items.content: Police commandos entered the building where a senator wanted by the International Criminal Court had sought refuge.

items.title: Gunshots fired in standoff at Philippine Senate over ICC suspect
items.standfirst: Gunshots broke out at the Philippine Senate on Wednesday and people ran for cover after a top politician wanted by the International Criminal Court said his arrest was imminent and security forces entered the building. Senator Ronald ​dela Rosa, a former ‌police chief who was the main enforcer of ex-Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte's bloody "war on drugs", had earlier urged people to mobilise to prevent his ⁠arrest and handover to the ICC. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more.
items.content: Gunshots broke out at the Philippine Senate on Wednesday and people ran for cover after a top politician wanted by the International Criminal Court said his arrest was imminent and security forces entered the building. Senator Ronald ​dela Rosa, a former ‌police chief who was the main enforcer of ex-Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte's bloody "war on drugs", had earlier urged people to mobilise to prevent his ⁠arrest and handover to the ICC. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more.

items.title: Shots fired in Philippine senate where senator accused of crimes against humanity was hiding
items.standfirst: Gunfire broke out in the Philippine senate where a senator is holed up trying to avoid arrest over claims of crimes against humanity.
items.content: Gunfire broke out in the Philippine senate where a senator is holed up trying to avoid arrest over claims of crimes against humanity.

items.title: Gunshots fired in Philippines senate in standoff with senator wanted by ICC
items.standfirst: Ronald dela Rosa, accused of crimes against humanity by international criminal court, has holed up in building to evade arrestGunshots have been fired in the Philippines senate as a senator who is wanted by the international criminal court (ICC) remained holed up in the building to evade arrest.Ronald dela Rosa, a Philippine senator accused of crimes against humanity for his role in overseeing the former president Rodrigo Duterte’s “war on drugs”, has spent two nights in the country’s senate in a standoff with the authorities. Continue reading...
items.content: Ronald dela Rosa, accused of crimes against humanity by international criminal court, has holed up in building to evade arrestGunshots have been fired in the Philippines senate as a senator who is wanted by the international criminal court (ICC) remained holed up in the building to evade arrest.Ronald dela Rosa, a Philippine senator accused of crimes against humanity for his role in overseeing the former president Rodrigo Duterte’s “war on drugs”, has spent two nights in the country’s senate in a standoff with the authorities. Continue reading...

items.title: Gunfire breaks out in Philippine Senate as police try to arrest senator
items.standfirst: Witnesses say a burst of gunfire has rung out in the Philippine Senate where authorities have tried to arrest a senator who is wanted by the ICC.
items.content: Witnesses say a burst of gunfire has rung out in the Philippine Senate where authorities have tried to arrest a senator who is wanted by the ICC.(Image credit: Aaron Favila)

clusters.title: Key points ahead of Trump meeting with Xi Jinping
clusters.neutral_headline: Key points ahead of Trump meeting with Xi Jinping
bullet_summary: President Trump said before he left that he and President Xi Jinping "have a lot of things to discuss
Reported by CBS News, BBC World, France24 and others

items.title: Key points ahead of Trump meeting with Xi Jinping
items.standfirst: President Trump arrived in Beijing on Wednesday for a high-stakes meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping. CBS News' Anna Coren and Nancy Cordes have more.
items.content: President Trump arrived in Beijing on Wednesday for a high-stakes meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping. CBS News' Anna Coren and Nancy Cordes have more.

items.title: Trade, Iran and Taiwan on the agenda as Trump arrives in China for high-stakes talks with Xi
items.standfirst: The US president says his first request to Xi would be to "open up" China, while Beijing is expected to press Trump on the status of Taiwan.
items.content: The US president says his first request to Xi would be to "open up" China, while Beijing is expected to press Trump on the status of Taiwan.

items.title: Trump arrives in China for historic trip
items.standfirst: US President Donald #Trump has arrived in Beijing for highly anticipated talks with his Chinese counterpart #Xi Jinping. The two leaders are expected to discuss the #Iran war, trade and #US arms sales to #Taiwan. The visit to #China is the first from a US leader since 2017.
items.content: US President Donald #Trump has arrived in Beijing for highly anticipated talks with his Chinese counterpart #Xi Jinping. The two leaders are expected to discuss the #Iran war, trade and #US arms sales to #Taiwan. The visit to #China is the first from a US leader since 2017.

items.title: Trump arrives in China as Iran war looms over summit with Xi Jinping
items.standfirst: President Trump touched down in Beijing on Wednesday ahead of his summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. CBS News' Courtney Kealy and Ramy Inocencio have the latest.
items.content: President Trump touched down in Beijing on Wednesday ahead of his summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. CBS News' Courtney Kealy and Ramy Inocencio have the latest.

items.title: Trump arrives in Beijing to meet with Xi Jinping
items.standfirst: President Trump said before he left that he and President Xi Jinping "have a lot of things to discuss."
items.content: President Trump said before he left that he and President Xi Jinping "have a lot of things to discuss."

items.title: Trump demands Xi ‘open’ China to US business
items.standfirst: US president arrives in Beijing for two-day summit clouded by geopolitical tensions and conflict over trade and tech
items.content: US president arrives in Beijing for two-day summit clouded by geopolitical tensions and conflict over trade and tech

items.title: Trade, Taiwan and Iran cast shadows on Trump’s China summit with Xi
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump will begin a two-day summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing on Thursday as the two countries engage in a fierce trade war and the U.S. conflict with Iran rages on.
items.content: President Donald Trump will begin a two-day summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing on Thursday as the two countries engage in a fierce trade war and the U.S. conflict with Iran rages on.

clusters.title: US Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve chair, replacing Jerome Powell
clusters.neutral_headline: US Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve chair, replacing Jerome Powell
bullet_summary: Kevin Warsh was confirmed by the Senate on Wednesday as the next Federal Reserve chair
Reported by Guardian World, CBS News, NPR News and others

items.title: US Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve chair, replacing Jerome Powell
items.standfirst: Warsh will serve four-year term as chair, taking over amid rising inflation and pressure from Trump to lower ratesThe US Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh as chair of the Federal Reserve, one of the most powerful roles in the federal government that holds enormous sway over the economy.The 54-45 Senate vote on Wednesday was split along party lines, with the exception of Democratic senator John Fetterman from Pennsylvania, who joined the Republican majority. It was most divisive confirmation vote for the position in history. Continue reading...
items.content: Warsh will serve four-year term as chair, taking over amid rising inflation and pressure from Trump to lower ratesThe US Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh as chair of the Federal Reserve, one of the most powerful roles in the federal government that holds enormous sway over the economy.The 54-45 Senate vote on Wednesday was split along party lines, with the exception of Democratic senator John Fetterman from Pennsylvania, who joined the Republican majority. It was most divisive confirmation vote for the position in history. Continue reading...

items.title: Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as Fed chair as Trump presses for lower rates
items.standfirst: The Senate voted to confirm Kevin Warsh as chairman of the Federal Reserve, marking a victory for President Trump.
items.content: The Senate voted to confirm Kevin Warsh as chairman of the Federal Reserve, marking a victory for President Trump.

items.title: Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as next Fed chair
items.standfirst: Kevin Warsh was confirmed by the Senate on Wednesday as the next Federal Reserve chair. Warsh will succeed Jerome Powell, whose term ends Friday. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns has more.
items.content: Kevin Warsh was confirmed by the Senate on Wednesday as the next Federal Reserve chair. Warsh will succeed Jerome Powell, whose term ends Friday. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns has more.

items.title: Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as next chair of the Federal Reserve
items.standfirst: Warsh has argued there's room for the central bank to lower interest rates, but that could be challenging at a time of rising inflation.
items.content: Warsh has argued there's room for the central bank to lower interest rates, but that could be challenging at a time of rising inflation. (Image credit: Andrew Harnik)

items.title: Kevin Warsh confirmed as new US Federal Reserve chair amid controversy
items.standfirst: Senate confirms Trump pick Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve chair amid growing concerns over independence.
items.content: Senate confirms Trump pick Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve chair amid growing concerns over independence.

clusters.title: Russia unleashes massive drone barrage on Ukraine killing six
clusters.neutral_headline: Russia unleashes massive drone barrage on Ukraine killing six
bullet_summary: Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelensky said more than 800 drones were launched, accusing Russia of timing the attack to coincide with US President Donald Trump’s visit to China
Reported by Al Jazeera, France24, BBC World and others

items.title: Russia unleashes massive drone barrage on Ukraine killing six
items.standfirst: Russia has launched a massive daytime drone barrage across Ukraine, killing at least six people.
items.content: Russia has launched a massive daytime drone barrage across Ukraine, killing at least six people.

items.title: Russia unleashes deadly daylight drone blitz across Ukraine
items.standfirst: A sweeping daytime Russian drone assault killed at least six people and wounded dozens across Ukraine on Wednesday, marking a shift in Moscow’s bombardment tactics. Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelensky said more than 800 drones were launched, accusing Russia of timing the attack to coincide with US President Donald Trump’s visit to China.
items.content: A sweeping daytime Russian drone assault killed at least six people and wounded dozens across Ukraine on Wednesday, marking a shift in Moscow’s bombardment tactics. Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelensky said more than 800 drones were launched, accusing Russia of timing the attack to coincide with US President Donald Trump’s visit to China.

items.title: Deadly Russian drone attacks on Ukraine resume after ceasefire expires
items.standfirst: Six people have been killed after Zelensky warned of "more waves" of Russian strikes through Wednesday.
items.content: Six people have been killed after Zelensky warned of "more waves" of Russian strikes through Wednesday.

items.title: Russia targets Ukraine with more than 800 drones in deadly daytime assault
items.standfirst: Strikes killed at least six people as Moscow and Kyiv trade long-range attacks after brief ceasefireEurope live – latest updatesRussia targeted Ukraine with more than 800 drones in a large-scale daytime assault that killed at least six people on Wednesday, hours after a previous deadly barrage.The strikes came as Kyiv and Moscow traded long-range attacks after a brief ceasefire, and despite the latest suggestion from Donald Trump that the war could soon come to an end. Continue reading...
items.content: Strikes killed at least six people as Moscow and Kyiv trade long-range attacks after brief ceasefireEurope live – latest updatesRussia targeted Ukraine with more than 800 drones in a large-scale daytime assault that killed at least six people on Wednesday, hours after a previous deadly barrage.The strikes came as Kyiv and Moscow traded long-range attacks after a brief ceasefire, and despite the latest suggestion from Donald Trump that the war could soon come to an end. Continue reading...

“This visit has led to a historic breakthrough in relations between Israel and the UAE,” the Israeli prime minister’s office said in a statement on Wednesday night
Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu made a secret visit to the United Arab Emirates recently, his office confirmed Wednesday

clusters.title: South Carolina court tosses murder conviction in Alex Murdaugh case
clusters.neutral_headline: South Carolina court tosses murder conviction in Alex Murdaugh case
bullet_summary: Alex Murdaugh — the disgraced former lawyer serving a life term for the murders of his wife and son — will get a new trial in South Carolina, the state Supreme Court said on Wednesday
Reported by Al Jazeera, CBS News, BBC World and others

items.title: South Carolina court tosses murder conviction in Alex Murdaugh case
items.standfirst: The state's high court ruled that Murdaugh was denied a fair trial due to comments from a court clerk suggesting guilt.
items.content: The state's high court ruled that Murdaugh was denied a fair trial due to comments from a court clerk suggesting guilt.

items.title: Court overturns Alex Murdaugh's murder convictions, orders new trial
items.standfirst: Alex Murdaugh was convicted of his killing his wife and his son at the family's home in 2021.
items.content: Alex Murdaugh was convicted of his killing his wife and his son at the family's home in 2021.

items.content: The court has ordered a new trial over the June 2021 killings of Paul and Maggie Murdaugh.

items.title: Will the boycott of Eurovision have any impact?
items.standfirst: The decision by five nations to boycott the song contest comes amid Israel's genocide in Gaza.
items.content: The decision by five nations to boycott the song contest comes amid Israel's genocide in Gaza.

items.title: Five countries are boycotting the Eurovision Song Contest over Israel
items.standfirst: The 2026 Eurovision Song Contest is now officially underway with 35 nations performing in Vienna over the next few days. The annual contest finds itself facing geopolitical backlash over Israel's participation in the contest. NBC News' Claudio Lavanga reports on several countries planning to boycott this year's event and the heightened security presence.
items.content: The 2026 Eurovision Song Contest is now officially underway with 35 nations performing in Vienna over the next few days. The annual contest finds itself facing geopolitical backlash over Israel's participation in the contest. NBC News' Claudio Lavanga reports on several countries planning to boycott this year's event and the heightened security presence.

items.title: From boycotts to Boy George, Eurovision is back
items.standfirst: The Eurovision Song Contest is back in Vienna amid a boycott over Israel’s participation and voting changes.
items.content: The Eurovision Song Contest is back in Vienna amid a boycott over Israel’s participation and voting changes.

clusters.title: Wes Streeting prepares to launch leadership challenge against Keir Starmer
clusters.neutral_headline: Wes Streeting prepares to launch leadership challenge against Keir Starmer

items.title: Wes Streeting prepares to launch leadership challenge against Keir Starmer
items.standfirst: Health secretary’s move to force race sparks scramble on left of Labour for candidate to oppose himWes Streeting is preparing to launch a leadership challenge against Keir Starmer on Thursday if the health secretary can secure the support of enough MPs to trigger a contest.Streeting’s move to force a race has sparked a frantic scramble on the left of Labour to find a candidate to oppose him, with Ed Miliband and Angela Rayner both possible contenders. Continue reading...
items.content: Health secretary’s move to force race sparks scramble on left of Labour for candidate to oppose himWes Streeting is preparing to launch a leadership challenge against Keir Starmer on Thursday if the health secretary can secure the support of enough MPs to trigger a contest.Streeting’s move to force a race has sparked a frantic scramble on the left of Labour to find a candidate to oppose him, with Ed Miliband and Angela Rayner both possible contenders. Continue reading...

items.title: Keir Starmer sets out changes to education, health and courts in king’s speech
items.standfirst: King Charles unveils government agenda for the next year as PM faces leadership threat from within LabourKeir Starmer on Wednesday looked to reassert his authority over his restive party, unveiling his plans for the next parliamentary session even as speculation grew that he would be challenged for his job as soon as Thursday.Starmer announced his second King’s Speech as prime minister, promising a package of measures with bills to abolish NHS England, overhaul the provision of special educational needs teaching, limit trials by jury, introduce digital ID and end the leasehold system in England and Wales. Continue reading...
items.content: King Charles unveils government agenda for the next year as PM faces leadership threat from within LabourKeir Starmer on Wednesday looked to reassert his authority over his restive party, unveiling his plans for the next parliamentary session even as speculation grew that he would be challenged for his job as soon as Thursday.Starmer announced his second King’s Speech as prime minister, promising a package of measures with bills to abolish NHS England, overhaul the provision of special educational needs teaching, limit trials by jury, introduce digital ID and end the leasehold system in England and Wales. Continue reading...

items.title: UK PM Starmer clings on as leadership talk overshadows king's speech
items.standfirst: The pomp of a speech by King Charles III to Britain's parliament Wednesday failed to quell speculation that a senior government minister is poised to launch a leadership challenge against embattled Prime Minister Keir Starmer. The monarch's address opening a new parliamentary session was overshadowed by reports that allies of Health Secretary Wes Streeting had said he was preparing to resign to trigger a contest to oust Starmer from office.
items.content: The pomp of a speech by King Charles III to Britain's parliament Wednesday failed to quell speculation that a senior government minister is poised to launch a leadership challenge against embattled Prime Minister Keir Starmer. The monarch's address opening a new parliamentary session was overshadowed by reports that allies of Health Secretary Wes Streeting had said he was preparing to resign to trigger a contest to oust Starmer from office.

clusters.title: Japanese snack packages turning black-and-white as Iran war depletes ink supply
clusters.neutral_headline: Japanese snack packages turning black-and-white as Iran war depletes ink supply
bullet_summary: Calbee, whose potato chip brands in particular are known for brightly coloured bag designs, said 14 of its products would switch to monochrome branding by the end of May
Reported by NPR News, Guardian Business

items.title: Japanese snack packages turning black-and-white as Iran war depletes ink supply
items.standfirst: The packaging on some snacks is turning black-and-white, as the war in Iran disrupts the supply of an ingredient used in colored ink. Calbee's chips originally came in a bright-orange bag.
items.content: The packaging on some snacks is turning black-and-white, as the war in Iran disrupts the supply of an ingredient used in colored ink. Calbee's chips originally came in a bright-orange bag.(Image credit: AP)

items.title: Iran war oil shortage forces Japan snack giant to use black-and-white packaging
items.standfirst: Calbee to switch its brightly coloured packaging to black and white because war has disrupted supply of certain raw materials used in inkJapan’s biggest snack maker has been forced to use black-and-white packaging for some flagship products because of ink ingredient shortages caused by the strait of Hormuz blockade.Calbee, whose potato chip brands in particular are known for brightly coloured bag designs, said 14 of its products would switch to monochrome branding by the end of May. Continue reading...
items.content: Calbee to switch its brightly coloured packaging to black and white because war has disrupted supply of certain raw materials used in inkJapan’s biggest snack maker has been forced to use black-and-white packaging for some flagship products because of ink ingredient shortages caused by the strait of Hormuz blockade.Calbee, whose potato chip brands in particular are known for brightly coloured bag designs, said 14 of its products would switch to monochrome branding by the end of May. Continue reading...
